R3 routing: ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry for 172.16.0.0/24 fwd-list fwd=10.0.0.10 (from R3 running-config)
R1 Serial0/1/1 ip 10.0.0.10 and R3 Serial0/1/0 ip 10.0.0.9 (interface IPs that match the reciprocal next-hops)
router2: device unreachable - All connection attempts failed (tool fetch errors for running-config, OSPF, DHCP)

missing_info: Which device/network is authoritative for 172.16.0.0/24 (which router/switch is 172.16.0.12 attached to)?
Provide router2's running-config / OSPF / routing table (management access currently failing) to confirm whether it owns or advertises 172.16.0.0/24.

plan_history.intent_description: 172.16.0.12 is unreachable because router1 and router3 have reciprocal forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 pointing at each other, creating a forwarding loop/blackhole; goal is to remove the erroneous entries so traffic can reach the authoritative router for 172.16.0.0/24.
plan_history.problem: 172.16.0.12 unreachable due to reciprocal ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entries on router1 and router3 pointing at each other (forwarding loop/blackhole).
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect ip-route-interface-forwarding-list/static forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 on router1 and router3 that point to each other. Operational risk: traffic to 172.16.0.0/24 may be dropped until the correct route is present or router2 advertises the network.

remaining_issues: router2 is still unreachable to management/collection (all connection attempts failed). Confirm router2 configuration and OSPF/routing to ensure the fix is permanent and that router2 is not reintroducing incorrect routes.

Confirm which device is authoritative for 172.16.0.0/24 (which device/network 172.16.0.12 is attached to) and validate routing advertisement/origin so the network remains reachable after device reboots/changes.
